title: The Most Dangerous Microbiome in Your Body Isn't in Your Gut. It's in Your Mouth.
description: While the world obsessed over gut bacteria, a quieter microbial ecosystem was seeding inflammation in hearts, joints, and brains. A new synthesis of 30+ systematic reviews confirms the oral microbiome as a mechanistically plausible driver of cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, Alzheimer's, and rheumatoid arthritis — and the pathogen implicated in Alzheimer's has been found in brain tissue. Correlation isn't causation, but 72 square centimeters of inflamed gum tissue bleeding into your bloodstream daily is not a metaphor. Your dentist may be your most underrated physician.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for The Most Dangerous Microbiome in Your Body Isn't in Your Gut. It's in Your Mouth.
